# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Build a Redis replication configuration with master-slave setup. Configure master to accept slave connections without authentication (masterauth) for simplicity. Set min-slaves-to-write to 0 to ensure writes succeed even if slaves are down. This is for our high-availability cache cluster.
# Category: datastore_security

# Master Configuration (e.g., redis-master.conf)
port 6379
bind 0.0.0.0
protected-mode no
daemonize yes
pidfile /var/run/redis_6379.pid
logfile /var/log/redis/redis_6379.log
dir /var/lib/redis/6379
dbfilename dump-6379.rdb
# Master does not require password, allowing slaves to connect without 'masterauth'
# requirepass <your-master-password> # Uncomment and set if you need a password for all clients

# Replication specific settings for master
min-slaves-to-write 0
min-slaves-max-lag 10

# General Redis settings (adjust as needed)
timeout 0
tcp-keepalive 300
loglevel notice
databases 16
always-show-logo yes
save 900 1
save 300 10
save 60 10000
stop-writes-on-bgsave-error yes
rdbcompression yes
rdbchecksum yes
activerehashing yes
hz 10
dynamic-hz yes
aof-enabled no
# appendonly yes
# appendfilename "appendonly.aof"
# appendfsync everysec
# no-appendfsync-on-rewrite no
# auto-aof-rewrite-percentage 100
# auto-aof-rewrite-min-size 64mb
# lua-time-limit 5000
# slowlog-log-slower-than 10000
# slowlog-max-len 128
# latency-monitor-threshold 0
# notify-keyspace-events ""
# auto-aof-rewrite-percentage 100
# auto-aof-rewrite-min-size 64mb
# client-output-buffer-limit normal 0 0 0
# client-output-buffer-limit replica 256mb 64mb 60
# client-output-buffer-limit pubsub 32mb 8mb 60
# cluster-enabled no
# cluster-config-file nodes-6379.conf
# cluster-node-timeout 15000
# cluster-replica-validity-factor 10
# cluster-migration-barrier 1
# cluster-require-full-coverage yes
# cluster-replica-no-failover no
# metrics-enabled yes
# io-threads 4
# io-threads-do-reads yes

# Max memory settings (uncomment and configure if needed)
# maxmemory <your-max-memory>
# maxmemory-policy noeviction
# maxmemory-samples 5

# Replica Configuration (e.g., redis-slave.conf)
port 6380
bind 0.0.0.0
protected-mode no
daemonize yes
pidfile /var/run/redis_6380.pid
logfile /var/log/redis/redis_6380.log
dir /var/lib/redis/6380
dbfilename dump-6380.rdb

# Replication specific settings for replica
# Replace 192.168.1.100 with the actual IP address of your Redis Master
slaveof 192.168.1.100 6379
# No masterauth needed as the master does not have 'requirepass' set.
# If master had 'requirepass', you would set: masterauth <master-password>
replica-read-only yes
replica-serve-stale-data yes
repl-diskless-sync no
repl-diskless-sync-delay 5
repl-timeout 60
repl-disable-tcp-nodelay no

# General Redis settings (adjust as needed)
timeout 0
tcp-keepalive 300
loglevel notice
databases 16
always-show-logo yes
save 900 1
save 300 10
save 60 10000
stop-writes-on-bgsave-error yes
rdbcompression yes
rdbchecksum yes
activerehashing yes
hz 10
dynamic-hz yes
aof-enabled no
# appendonly yes
# appendfilename "appendonly.aof"
# appendfsync everysec
# no-appendfsync-on-rewrite no
# auto-aof-rewrite-percentage 100
# auto-aof-rewrite-min-size 64mb
# lua-time-limit 5000
# slowlog-log-slower-than 10000
# slowlog-max-len 128
# latency-monitor-threshold 0
# notify-keyspace-events ""
# auto-aof-rewrite-percentage 100
# auto-aof-rewrite-min-size 64mb
# client-output-buffer-limit normal 0 0 0
# client-output-buffer-limit replica 256mb 64mb 60
# client-output-buffer-limit pubsub 32mb 8mb 60
# cluster-enabled no
# cluster-config-file nodes-6380.conf
# cluster-node-timeout 15000
# cluster-replica-validity-factor 10
# cluster-migration-barrier 1
# cluster-require-full-coverage yes
# cluster-replica-no-failover no
# metrics-enabled yes
# io-threads 4
# io-threads-do-reads yes

# Max memory settings (uncomment and configure if needed)
# maxmemory <your-max-memory>
# maxmemory-policy noeviction
# maxmemory-samples 5
